commit.message:
feat: Cozy visual redesign P0 — light BG, rounded tiles, grass paths, parchment UI, pill buttons

Sabrina's cozy spec implemented:
- Scene BG: dark green → warm parchment/seasonal sky
- Tiles: sharp rectangles → 6px rounded corners
- Grid gaps → grass paths (0xA8C88A)
- UI panels: dark → warm cream (0xFAF3E8)
- Buttons: sharp → pill shape (height/2 radius)
- Text: light-on-dark → warm brown on parchment
- HUD: block → 32px horizontal strip
- Plant sway: 1.2Hz → 0.7Hz (more natural)
- Soil colors: cold grey-brown → warm sand

commit.message:
fix: plants now VISIBLE — 3x bigger sizes, no invisible start, emoji labels, contrast colors

Root cause: PLANT_SIZE_SEED was 8px with 0.45 scale = 3.6px (invisible).
Now: SEED=20px, SPROUT=28px, GROWING=36px, MATURE=44px.
Scales: SEED=0.6, SPROUT=0.75, GROWING=0.9, MATURE=1.0.
Plants start at full target scale instead of 0.01 (invisible).
Emoji labels at fontSize 28 (was 14, invisible at 0.45 scale).
Dark outlines on all plant shapes for soil contrast.
Planted tiles use subtle brown border instead of green overlay.

Verified via screenshots: 3 plants clearly visible on grid tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: plant visibility — contrast colors, dark outlines, emoji labels

FIX 1: Planted tiles no longer use green overlay (green-on-green = invisible).
Planted tile overlay changed from green ring to subtle dark brown border.
The plant shape itself provides all the color feedback.

FIX 2: All plant shapes now get a 2px dark brown outline (0x2d1a0e) drawn
after the shape, making plants pop against any soil background.

FIX 3: Emoji labels (🍅🥕🌻 etc.) appear above seed/sprout stage plants
for immediate identification. Labels auto-remove once plants reach
growing stage and are visually distinct on their own.

fix: enlarge plants, draw gardener character, add tile variation

P0: Increase plant visual sizes — bump PLANT_SIZE_MATURE from 24 to 28,
raise SEED/SPROUT keyframe scales (0.5→0.65, 0.65→0.8), increase root
and bush shape multipliers, set all stage alphas to 1.0 for bold colors.

featuring green overalls, skin-toned head, straw hat with red band,
eyes with pupils, and a smile.

specks, and occasional soil scratch marks for natural texture.

commit.message:
fix: plants visible, player on grid, tile hover highlight

Bug A (Plants Invisible): Fixed double-scaling in getShapeData() where
keyframe.scale was applied to both shape size AND container scale,
making seeds ~1px. Now shapes draw at full baseSize and container
scale alone handles growth stages. Bumped PLANT_SIZE_MATURE 14→24
and SEED keyframe scale 0.3→0.5 for clear visibility.

Bug B (Player Off Grid): Moved player container from scene container
to grid container so it transforms with grid scale/position.

Bug C (No Hover Effect): Fixed tile hover highlight hidden behind
opaque GridSystem tiles by re-rendering them as transparent when
TileRenderer is set. Added golden border stroke to hover highlight
for clearer visual feedback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: commit real build SHA to git so Dokploy local builds get it

Dokploy builds the image from source (not GHCR), so build args and
env vars set at CI build time are irrelevant. The only reliable way is
to commit _build-sha.js with the real SHA before triggering the webhook,
so Dokploy pulls a repo that already has the SHA baked in as source code.

Uses [skip ci] to prevent infinite workflow loops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use GHCR image in docker-compose, not local build

Dokploy was building the image from source on the server, so
_build-sha.js was always the committed 'dev' fallback. Now it
pulls ghcr.io/johnturek/prompt-platform:latest with pull_policy=always.

commit.message:
fix: bust Docker GHA cache with CACHEBUST ARG before COPY

ARG CACHEBUST before COPY . . forces all layers from COPY onwards
to be rebuilt whenever the SHA changes, bypassing stale GHA cache.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: inject build SHA via generated JS module (not ARG/ENV)

CI overwrites src/views/_build-sha.js with the real SHA before docker
build. COPY picks it up as regular file content — no build-arg caching
issues. Health endpoint and footer both read from the same module.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: prompt-platform
repository.description: Prompt-a-thon event management platform for Copilot workshops

commit.message:
fix: use .version file for build SHA to avoid Docker layer cache bug

ARG/ENV baking can be served from GHA layer cache even when the value
changes. Writing the SHA to a .version file before docker build ensures
COPY . . always invalidates the layer and picks up the real SHA.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: tlsr8266_mesh
repository.description: Collection of tools and code for reprogramming Tuya BLE smart lights (TYBT1) boards based on the TLSR8266 MCU with new Rust firmware.

commit.message:
Fix BLE notification packet op/vendor_id order in rf_link_slave_add_status

The refactored HEAD version incorrectly placed vendor_id (2 bytes) before
op (1 byte) in the assembled notification payload, reversing the original
mesh packet layout where op precedes vendor_id. This caused Python clients
to read the vendor_id low byte (0x11) as the opcode instead of the actual
opcode (e.g. 0x1b for STATUS).

Restore the original order: op at dat[7], vendor_id at dat[8..10], par at
dat[10..20]. This matches the raw mesh packet memory layout that the
original memcpy-based implementation produced.

Add test_rf_link_slave_add_status_packet_layout to explicitly assert the
op-first, vendor_id-second layout expected by decrypt_notification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: tlsr8266_mesh
repository.description: Collection of tools and code for reprogramming Tuya BLE smart lights (TYBT1) boards based on the TLSR8266 MCU with new Rust firmware.

commit.message:
Fix rf_link_response_callback tests to match corrected dispatch

Tests were setting val[15] on the request packet (p_req), but after
fixing the match expression to read from the response packet (ppp),
the tests must set val[15] on the response instead — matching how
rf_drv_8266.rs sets pkt_light_status.val[15] before calling the
callback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: tlsr8266_mesh
repository.description: Collection of tools and code for reprogramming Tuya BLE smart lights (TYBT1) boards based on the TLSR8266 MCU with new Rust firmware.

commit.message:
Fix race condition in time driver and related bugs

- Fix race condition in clock_time64() where reading the hardware timer
  outside the critical section allowed interrupts to update the last-seen
  timestamp in between, causing false overflow detection and an
  instantaneous multi-minute jump in Instant::now(). This manifested as
  light transitions snapping abruptly to their final state. Hardware read
  is now performed inside the critical section so the read and comparison
  are atomic with respect to interrupts.

- Fix incorrect variable read in the BLE response callback match
  expression, which was reading from the request packet instead of the
  response packet being built.

- Wrap the mesh provisioning BLE connection in an EOFError handler to
  suppress spurious errors on device reset during disconnection. Also
  reduce the post-pairing monitoring window.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: tlsr8266_mesh

commit.message:
perf: cap particle burst + DRY garden update + GDD compliance audit

#324 Performance Audit:
- ParticleSystem.burst(): Cap burst count at 128 to prevent unbounded
  particle allocation exceeding pool capacity (256 max)
- GardenScene.update(): Deduplicate plant info panel code — two
  identical branches (27 lines) collapsed to single branch (14 lines)

#329 GDD Compliance Audit:
- Created comprehensive gap analysis in .squad/decisions/inbox/
- 19/21 features fully implemented (95%+ GDD compliance)
- 2 partial: spacing density penalty, second garden plot
- Phase 5 recommendations: fill gaps before new features

Systems verified clean:
- AnimationSystem: Map-based tween cleanup, zero leak risk
- PlantRenderer: Full sprite caching, draw-on-change only
- FPSMonitor: Properly wired via GameLoop, dev-mode only

commit.message:
fix: harvest via performAction emits plant:harvested

Route HARVEST tool through PlantSystem.harvestPlant() instead of the
generic tool execute path. The tool config's execute() was calling
plant.harvest() directly without emitting plant:harvested or removing
the plant from PlantSystem. Both executeToolOnCurrentTile() and
performTestAction() now handle HARVEST the same way SEED is handled.

Also tracks harvestedSeeds for the results screen summary.

Build: zero TS errors, clean Vite production build.

commit.message:
fix: intercept ESM import of node:sqlite in ESM loader to fix CLI subprocess crash on Node.js v20 (#77)

* Initial plan

* fix: intercept ESM import of node:sqlite in ESM loader to fix CLI subprocess crash on Node.js v20

The CJS polyfill (node-sqlite-polyfill.cjs) patched Module._load but ESM
imports bypass Module._load entirely, going through the ESM builtinStrategy
which threw ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE for node:sqlite on Node.js v20.

- Add scripts/sqlite-polyfill-esm.mjs: ESM shim exporting DatabaseSync
  backed by better-sqlite3 (API-compatible with node:sqlite)
- Update scripts/esm-ext-loader.mjs: intercept ESM 'node:sqlite' specifier
  in resolve hook on Node.js < v22.5.0 and redirect to the shim file
- Update agentic-demo.yml: document ESM node:sqlite interception in comments
- Update copilot-compat-test.yml: add sqlite-polyfill-esm.mjs to paths
  trigger and add ESM node:sqlite test step

Co-authored-by: rleme <4821673+rleme@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: use .slice(0,2) when parsing Node.js version in ESM loader

Co-authored-by: rleme <4821673+rleme@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: rleme <4821673+rleme@users.noreply.github.com>

  instead of fragile mouse clicks; strict expect() assertions replace console.warn fallbacks
- Rewrite 'Day advancement' test: use movePlayer + performAction pattern;
  strict assertion that actions are consumed; verify day auto-advances
- Add new 'Complete plant->water cycle proves actions work (#298)' test:
  full seed->water cycle with strict assertions proving founder concern resolved
- All tests use performAction() hook (PixiJS events don't propagate from
  synthetic Playwright mouse events); matches Multi-day test pattern

commit.message:
feat(e2e): add multi-day garden playthrough test (#299)

Add comprehensive Playwright E2E test that plays through 4 in-game days:
- Plants seeds on empty tiles each day using selectTool + performAction hooks
- Attempts watering of planted tiles
- Uses rest() to advance days
- Validates day increments, plant creation, event firing
- Takes screenshots at key moments to playtest-screenshots/
- Verifies no JS errors and game responsiveness after multi-day run

Also adds new test hooks to support direct game interaction:
- performAction(): execute current tool on player's tile
- movePlayer(row, col): instantly reposition player
- rest(): consume remaining actions and advance day

These hooks bypass PixiJS's event system which doesn't process
synthetic browser events from Playwright.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: unique procedural visuals for all 22 plants (#285)

Replace generic PixiJS primitives with per-plant distinct silhouettes.
Each of the 22 species now has a unique mature drawing dispatched by plantId:

- Flowers: sunflower (disk+petals), lavender (spike), orchid (sepals+lip),
  golden marigold (pom-pom), moonflower (star+glow)
- Herbs: basil (dome leaves), mint (paired serrated), sage (fuzzy alternate)
- Vegetables: tomato (fruit+calyx), pepper (bell+ridges)
- Root vegs: carrot (tapered+fronds), radish (bulb+root tip)
- Vines: pea (pods+tendrils), cucumber (striped+vine curl)
- Berries: blueberry (clusters+crowns), strawberry (hearts+seeds)
- Melons: heirloom squash (ribbed), watermelon (striped oval)
- Trees: frost willow (drooping branches+ice crystals)
- Exotic: venus flytrap (jaws+teeth), ghost pepper (wrinkled+fiery)

Changes:
- plantVisuals.ts: PlantFamily type + plantFamily field on all 22 defs
- PlantRenderer.ts drawMatureShape: per-plantId switch (22 unique drawings)
- PlantRenderer.ts drawGrowingShape: per-plantFamily switch (10 families)
- PlantRenderer.ts update: mature pulse animation (scale breathing)
- DiscoveryPopup/HUD: stubs for pre-existing build errors on main

All colors from plantVisuals.ts config constants.

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Eldad Hauzman <ehauzman@gmail.com>

feat: season selection & multi-season runs (closes #201)

- Season selector UI: 4 season cards with emoji, difficulty badge, hazard warnings, palette preview
- Season-specific seed pools: plants filtered by availableSeasons
- Multi-season run mode: unlocked at 30 runs, plays Spring→Summer→Fall→Winter (3 days each)
- Multi-season 2× score multiplier via ScoringSystem.setScoreMultiplier()
- Season preference persisted to localStorage
- Daily challenges disable season selector when active
- EventBus events: season:selected, multiseason:transition, multiseason:completed
- Unlock milestone: runs_30 for multi-season mode
- Player.resetDay() for multi-season transitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: stabilize sqlite cleanup accounting

Truncate SQLite WAL during cleanup and keep the public storage statistic focused on stable database size, while quota enforcement continues to account for transient sidecars separately. This preserves accurate quotas without regressing the legacy ExperienceStore cleanup behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add automated documentation deployment workflow (#13)

* ci: add Netlify deployment for docs site

Adds netlify.toml (build config) and a GitHub Actions workflow that:
- Deploys a preview on every PR touching docs/
- Deploys to production on push to main

Requires NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN and NETLIFY_SITE_ID repository secrets.
